other_observers: none; John Drummond or someone in his group of birders including Jeannie Mitchell, Kelly Shipe and others.
description: A warbler by bill shape and bright coloration. Bill longish. Chin and throat bright yellow. Black surrounding eye except white eye arcs, extending down to form a triangle of black on anterior part of auricular. White behind the black of face. White supercilium. Crown and back medium gray with two whitish wingbars. Belly white, and so are flanks but with broad dark verticle streaking.Behaviors: Slow foraging bird that explored limbs and trunk for insects. When it flew it moved only about 12 feet or less except when flock moved to another tree. Extracted unknown insect or mold from tree bark crack.Call: nonePlumage: 1st year
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
